stories.title: Trump Administration Asks OpenAI to Stagger AI Model Release
stories.summary: The Trump administration has asked OpenAI to stagger the release of an upcoming powerful artificial intelligence model, according to a person familiar with the matter, nearly two weeks after rival Anthropic PBC suspended its most capable offerings from the market under regulatory pressure.

stories.title: Trump Blocks Housing Bill Until SAVE Act is Passed | Balance of Power: Late Edition 06/25/2026
stories.summary: "Balance of Power: Late Edition" focuses on the intersection of politics and global business. On today's show, Rep. Bryan Steil, a Wisconsin Republican, says he believes the bipartisan housing bill President Trump delayed signing will ultimately be enacted, citing overwhelming support in Congress. Joe LaVorgna, SMBC Americas Chief Economist and former Counselor to Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent,

stories.title: LaVorgna: Fed May Need to Raise Rates
stories.summary: Joe LaVorgna, SMBC Americas Chief Economist and former Counselor to Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, says the Fed will not cut rates and may need to raise them as PCE inflation rises to a three-year high. LaVorgna says a resilient labor market could lead workers to press for higher wages, adding to inflation pressure, and calls Trump’s Fed chair pick Kevin Warsh “a great pick.” He speaks with Kai

stories.title: ON Semiconductor strikes $7 billion deal for Synaptics in physical AI push
stories.summary: ON Semiconductor said the deal bumps up its total addressable market by $30 billion, to $243 billion by 2030.

stories.title: Rewriting the Intelligence Map
stories.summary: Pak-Sun Ting, Co-founder and CEO of Votee AI, outlines how sovereign AI architecture and native language models aret reshaping enterprise governance while bringing billions of underserved language speakers into the generative AI era. stories.title: South Korea’s ‘Ant’ Army Is Driving an AI Market Frenzy
stories.summary: Some 14 million retail investors are piling into shares of two big tech giants, but worries are growing about what happens if chip demand dries up.

stories.title: Chicago Fed President Goolsbee says inflation is too high; Williams sees price pressures easing
stories.summary: In a live CNBC interview from his home district, Goolsbee declined to speculate on where he thinks interest rates are headed.

stories.title: Navigating the Leviathans: Professor John Mearsheimer and the resurgence of great power politics
stories.summary: In an era defined by shifting alliances and structural instability, the resurgence of great power politics has returned to the forefront of global strategic discourse. stories.title: Why South Korea’s AI Stock Mania Is a Warning
stories.summary: South Korea’s stock market has surged about 200% year-on-year, powered by an AI-fueled chip rally. But extreme volatility and heavy reliance on two companies may mean it’s a bubble waiting to burst. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Tenstorrent CEO on Next Leg of AI Tech, Deal Interest
stories.summary: Tenstorrent CEO Jim Keller discusses AI buildout and what’s next for chips. He speaks with Romaine Bostick and Katie Greifeld on “The Close.” (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.summary: "The only crop we're harvesting is what you planted: decades of mistrust," Iranian parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf said in a social media post.

stories.title: US Sanctions Rwandan Refinery in Congo Gold Smuggling Network
stories.summary: The US has sanctioned Rwanda’s Gasabo Gold Refinery Ltd. for fueling conflict by processing gold stolen from eastern Democratic Republic of Congo.

stories.title: Apple Supplier Lingyi Set for HK Debut After $1.1 Billion Float
stories.summary: Lingyi iTech Guangdong Co. a Chinese maker of electronic devices, is set to begin trading in Hong Kong on Friday after raising HK$8.3 billion ($1.06 billion) in a share sale, part of the city’s busiest month for listings this year.

stories.title: Indonesia Opens Door to Dirty Money to Fund Prabowo’s Plans
stories.summary: Indonesia is dangling unprecedented legal protections for investors in President Prabowo Subianto’s sovereign wealth fund, a move analysts warn could attract money with questionable origins and further erode the reputation of Southeast Asia’s largest economy.

stories.title: Iran’s Leaders Are in No Hurry to Get a Peace Deal
stories.summary: On today’s Big Take podcast, David Gura and Bloomberg Opinion’s Marc Champion reflect on the state of negotiations between the US and Iran, discuss why Tehran will likely emerge from the conflict in a better strategic position than before and what foreign powers – from the Gulf to China – will be watching for in a proposed settlement. Listen wherever you get your podcasts. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: India’s drone makers see orders surge for weaponised UAVs
stories.summary: Conflict in Iran pushes New Delhi to focus on domestic defence supply chains

stories.title: Apple Podcasts’ Video Push Shows Early Promise
stories.summary: Apple charged into video podcasts earlier this year after ceding market share to YouTube. Shows that opted in early are cautiously optimistic about the results.

stories.title: Apple stock gets slammed on bigger Mac, iPad price hikes. Why it can weather the storm
stories.summary: The stock had its worst session in more than a year as management made its first official move to pass higher memory costs onto consumers.

Rowe Price Co-Portfolio Manager Dynamic Global Bond Strategy Adam Marden, JPMorgan Asset Management Fixed Income Portfolio Manager Stephanie Doyle, and BNP Paribas Global Head of Credit Strategy & Desk Analyst Viktor Hjort. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Oil reverses losses, rises after an Iran attack on cargo vessel
stories.summary: Oil prices turned around and rose Thursday, hitting their session highs in late-day trading after Iran attacked a vessel on the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Google to Lose High-Profile AI Staffers to Rival Anthropic
stories.summary: Two leading AI researchers at Google are planning to leave for rival Anthropic, according to sources, adding to a series of high-profile departures that risk undercutting the search giant’s position in AI. Bloomberg's Julia Love joins Ed Ludlow on "Bloomberg Tech." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Qualcomm CEO on Data Center Chips, Adding Meta as a Customer
stories.summary: Qualcomm says its expansion into chips for data centers will produce “billions” in revenue in the fiscal year that begins in October, suggesting the company is gaining a foothold in the booming market for AI gear. Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon joins Bloomberg's Ed Ludlow and Romaine Bostick on "Bloomberg Tech." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Luxury spending now driven by experiences and 'inheritourism'
stories.summary: While luxury goods sales are expected to grow between 1% and 4%, experiences are on track for growth of between 3% and 7% this year, according to a new report.

stories.title: Bob Sloan's Market Positioning Warning
stories.summary: Markets may be near record highs, but Bob Sloan, S3 Partners Founder & Managing Partner says the real story is happening beneath the surface. He joins Bloomberg Open Interest to explain why passive investors are fueling AI stocks higher while hedge funds quietly sell into the rally and why tracking investor positioning may be just as important as earnings or valuations. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Micron's AI Pricing Power Explained
stories.summary: Micron's pricing power may have permanently changed the semiconductor industry. D.A. Davidson analyst Gil Luria joined Bloomberg Open Interest to explain why AI has turned memory into a strategic asset and how Apple could actually emerge stronger despite rising chip costs. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.summary: The US wants to reach a deal with Iran to end the war but won’t do so “at any price,” Secretary of State Marco Rubio said on Thursday, as he reiterated that tolls in the Strait of Hormuz were unacceptable.

Sean Casten of Illinois says he is "very encouraged" House Speaker Mike Johnson will send President Donald Trump the bipartisan housing bill, arguing the move suggests Johnson is standing up to Trump as the president's poll numbers fall.
